title: بعثة اقتصادية برازيلية تحل بالمغرب لبحث فرص الشراكة والاستثمار
summary: تستعد بعثة اقتصادية برازيلية تضم ست شركات تنشط في مجالات متعددة للقيام بزيارة عمل إلى المغرب ما بين 10 و13 يونيو المقبل، بهدف استكشاف فرص الاستثمار وإقامة شراكات جديدة مع الفاعلين الاقتصاديين بالمملكة. وتأتي هذه الخطوة بمبادرة من الغرفة التجارية العربية البرازيلية، بشراكة مع سفارة المغرب فيأكمل الق…
body: تستعد بعثة اقتصادية برازيلية تضم ست شركات تنشط في مجالات متعددة للقيام بزيارة عمل إلى المغرب ما بين 10 و13 يونيو المقبل، بهدف استكشاف فرص الاستثمار وإقامة شراكات جديدة مع الفاعلين الاقتصاديين بالمملكة. وتأتي هذه الخطوة بمبادرة من الغرفة التجارية العربية البرازيلية، بشراكة مع سفارة المغرب في أكمل القراءة » المقالة بعثة اقتصادية برازيلية تحل بالمغرب لبحث فرص الشراكة والاستثمار fمنشورة على جريدة الصباح .

title: How Hot Is It Really at the French Open? Check the Wet Bulb Globe Temperature
summary: Meet the wet bulb globe temperature, the ominous measure that shows when it's too hot to go outside.

title: 'Poised to disintegrate': Antarctica's 'Doomsday Glacier' is set to lose its ice shelf this year
summary: West Antarctica's "Doomsday Glacier" is on the brink of losing its ice shelf, further compromising the already melting ice mass and threatening to unleash devastating sea-level rises.

title: Scientists detect 'naked' black hole that's heavier than its host galaxy ‪—‬ pointing to exotic new processes in the early universe
summary: Astronomers weighed a black hole in a "little red dot" discovered by the James Webb telescope. They found it to be so overmassive that it may have formed before its host galaxy had a chance to develop.

title: A New Species of Tiny Octopus Was Discovered in the Galápagos Islands
summary: An octopus about the size of a golf ball was first spotted in 2015 near Darwin Island. A new study gives it both a formal description and a name.

title: Memory on trial: the new science of when to trust eyewitness testimony
summary:
Nature, Published online: 27 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01618-1

Innocent people are frequently convicted of crimes on the basis of a witness’s false memories. Emerging science poses a surprising solution.

title: Brilliant-green fireball meteor explodes over erupting volcano in the Philippines
summary: Multiple videos show a rare "fireball" meteor breaking apart with a flash of emerald light over the erupting Mount Mayon. Initial reports suggested that the falling space rock hit the volcano, but this was untrue.

summary:

title: Toxic plant on Ming dynasty-era surgical tools may be world’s oldest chemical evidence of topical anesthetic
summary: An analysis of residue on centuries-old surgical tools reveals the use of a toxic anesthetic in Ming dynasty-era Chinese medicine.

title: Bizarre patterns on Venus have scientists puzzled
summary: Scientists are trying to understand Venus' bright surface formations, called coronae, using new 3D maps.

title: Bead net funerary shroud: A 2,500-year-old beaded veil from Egypt depicting the deceased's transformation into Osiris
summary: This funerary shroud was made from thousands of multicolored beads and woven to represent a human face and a large scarab beetle.

title: Catapult the cow! 6 medieval castles that were never conquered
summary: Many medieval castles were formidable stone fortifications. Live Science takes a look at six that were never conquered.

title: China launches 'human artificial embryos' to space in bid to see whether reproduction is possible off-world
summary: China's Tianzhou-10 mission just delivered embryo-like structures made from living stem cells to the Tiangong space station. Experiments could shed light on how radiation and microgravity affect human reproduction.

title: Mars looks blue and bruised in surreal new images from Psyche spacecraft — Space photo of the week
summary: On its way to a metal asteroid, NASA's Psyche probe tested its cameras as it got a gravity assist from the Red Planet.

title: How hot is Earth's core?
summary: What's the temperature in Earth's core, and how did we figure that out?

title: One of Neptune's 16 moons is not like the others, James Webb telescope finds — and it could be key to fully understanding the solar system
summary: Neptune has a complicated life story, and its moon Nereid might be the only one left standing from the planet’s multibillion-year history.

title: Vanishing tongues and life on Mars: Books in brief
summary:

title: There's a new T. rex from the dinosaur age — and it ruled the seas with a skull-crushing bite
summary: The newly described mosasaur Tylosaurus rex spanned up to 43 feet (13 meters) long and may have been one of the fiercest marine predators of the dinosaur age.

title: 'I have no doubt that life is out there': Why radio astronomers are convinced alien contact is only a matter of time
summary: In the book "The Echoing Universe: How Radio Astronomy Helps Us See the Invisible Cosmos," astrophysicist Emma Chapman describes the hidden corners of space that only radio waves can reveal — and makes the case for contacting aliens.

title: Great Pyramid of Giza is remarkably resilient to earthquakes —‬ and it's due to the ancient Egyptians' 'extraordinary' engineering knowledge
summary: The Great Pyramid of Giza has survived for more than 4,600 years despite nearby earthquakes, and new research reveals why.

title: Complex animals evolved up to 10 million years earlier than previously thought, fossil discovery shows
summary: Trove of fossils discovered in Canada sheds light on "when life first became large, complex and unmistakenly animal."

title: Physicists confirm 'negative time' is real by asking the atoms themselves
summary: A new experiment confirms that photons passing through a cloud of atoms can spend a negative amount of time there, and the atoms themselves are the ones saying so.

title: The story behind the first British person in space
summary: It's 35 years since Helen Sharman boarded a Soviet spacecraft heading into orbit

title: Celestron AstroMaster LT 70AZ review
summary: A simple, beginner-friendly telescope that makes it easy to get started and enjoy your first real views of the moon and planets.

title: بيان رسمي مصري بعد حادثة اصطياد نوع نادر من أسماك القرش
summary: أصدر المعهد القومي المصري لعلوم البحار والمصائد، السبت، بيانا بشأن حادثة اصطياد نوع نادر من أسماك القرش في منطقة القصير بالبحر الأحمر.

title: دراسة تكشف "سر مثلث برمودا" الذي حير العلماء
summary: كشف علماء من مؤسسة كارنيغي للعلوم وجامعة ييل، عن تفسير جديد للغز الجيولوجي المرتبط بجزر برمودا، بعد اكتشاف بنية صخرية ضخمة مخفية تحت قاع المحيط الأطلسي ساهمت في إبقاء الجزيرة مرتفعة فوق سطح البحر منذ ملايين السنين.
